articles.full_text:
Andhra Pradesh, Telangana Weather LATEST Update: Scorching Heat to Continue, But Rain May Cool Telugu States
Andhra Pradesh, Telangana Weather LATEST Update: After days of intense heat and rising humidity, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana may finally get some relief as weather officials predict light to moderate rainfall, thunderstorms and gusty winds
Rain Likely Across Telangana
The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre has said that changing weather conditions could trigger light to moderate rainfall in several Telangana districts over the next three days. Thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds reaching speeds of 30-40 kmph may also occur in isolated places.
While temperatures are expected to remain stable for the next two days, weather experts believe the mercury could gradually rise by 2 to 4 degrees Celsius later this week. Despite the brief spell of rain, hot and humid conditions are likely to continue in many areas.
Andhra Pradesh May Witness Thunderstorms
According to the Amaravati Meteorological Centre, northeasterly and southerly winds are active over the lower troposphere across Andhra Pradesh and Yanam. Due to this, scattered rainfall and thunderstorms are expected in several coastal and Rayalaseema districts.
North Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Yanam may receive isolated showers along with lightning and strong winds of up to 40-50 kmph. Similar weather activity is expected in South Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Rayalaseema, where thunderstorms and isolated lightning strikes are also likely.
Heatwave Conditions Continue Despite Rain Chances
Even though rain activity is expected in parts of the Telugu states, the overall heatwave situation is unlikely to ease completely. Temperatures in many districts have already crossed the 40-degree mark, leading to uncomfortable weather conditions because of high humidity levels.
Officials say maximum temperatures across Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Yanam and Rayalaseema are expected to remain mostly unchanged over the next five days. However, the expected showers could provide temporary relief from the scorching summer heat.

articles.title: Telangana Braces For Dangerous Heatwave As Temperatures May Touch 47°C - TeluguBulletin.com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Telangana is preparing for another intense spell of extreme summer heat, with weather updates indicating that severe heatwave conditions are likely to return from May 12 onward. After a brief four-day pause in high temperatures, the state is expected to witness Heatwave Phase 3.0, bringing dangerous daytime temperatures across several districts.
According to the latest weather update dated May 10, the southwest monsoon is expected to make its onset over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands around May 15, with a possible variation of one day. However, despite the monsoon progress in the Bay of Bengal region, Telangana is unlikely to receive major immediate relief from the ongoing heat conditions.
Forecasters warn that temperatures in North and East Telangana districts could rise sharply over the coming days. Several regions are expected to record temperatures close to 46°C, while Hyderabad may cross the 43°C mark during the peak afternoon hours.
The most critical period is expected between May 18 and May 22, when dry low-level winds may push temperatures even higher across eastern Telangana districts. Some isolated areas could reportedly touch a dangerous 47°C, raising concerns over prolonged exposure to heat and worsening outdoor conditions.
Weather experts are also warning that the heatwave may continue for more than 10 consecutive days starting from May 12, making it one of the longest and most severe heat spells of the season so far. Northern and eastern parts of Telangana are expected to face the harshest impact, with strong hot winds likely during daytime hours.
Although a few scattered rain spells are possible during this period, they are not expected to bring significant cooling or long-lasting relief. The overall weather pattern is still expected to remain dominated by dry and extremely hot conditions across most parts of the state.
Residents are being advised to avoid direct sun exposure during afternoon hours, stay hydrated, and take necessary precautions as temperatures continue to rise rapidly across Telangana.

articles.title: Hyderabad Weather Report- May 10, 2026: Heat Continues As Evening Thunderstorm Chances Remain - NativePlanet
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad is expected to experience another intensely warm summer day on Sunday, May 10, 2026, with temperatures remaining high through the afternoon before slightly easing during the night hours. Weather conditions across the city point toward a combination of dry daytime heat, rising humidity, and the possibility of isolated evening thunderstorm activity linked to pre-monsoon developments over Telangana.
Temperature And Day Conditions
The city is likely to record a minimum temperature of around 25-26 degrees Celsius, while daytime highs may reach between 36 and 37 degrees Celsius in several parts of Hyderabad.
From late morning onward, temperatures are expected to rise rapidly under mostly sunny skies. Areas exposed to direct sunlight for longer durations, especially central urban zones with dense construction, may feel considerably hotter than the actual recorded temperature. The "feels like" temperature could remain uncomfortable during peak afternoon hours because of humidity levels staying close to 80 percent in some pockets.
Neighbourhoods with heavy traffic movement and concrete concentration, including parts of Secunderabad, Begumpet, Punjagutta, Ameerpet, Kukatpally, Madhapur, Gachibowli and the Financial District, are likely to experience stronger heat retention through the day.
Evening Weather Shift Expected
Although most of the day is expected to remain dry, meteorological patterns suggest that conditions may begin changing later in the evening. Gusty winds and isolated thunderstorm activity could develop in parts of Hyderabad due to ongoing pre-monsoon atmospheric instability over the Deccan region.
Cloud build-up may increase after sunset, especially toward the western and northwestern sections of the city. Areas on the outskirts, including Patancheru, Miyapur, Kompally, Shamshabad and Medchal, may witness brief wind activity or scattered light showers if storm cells strengthen during the evening hours.
At present, rainfall probability across the city remains low overall, but isolated weather disturbances cannot be ruled out completely.
Heat Impact Across Hyderabad
The afternoon heat is expected to remain the primary weather concern on May 10. Outdoor exposure between 12 pm and 4 pm could feel exhausting, particularly for commuters, delivery workers, construction labourers and people travelling on two-wheelers.
Road surfaces and elevated corridors are also expected to trap heat during the afternoon. Traffic-heavy stretches such as the Outer Ring Road approach zones, HITEC City corridors and major junctions in the central city may feel significantly warmer than surrounding suburban areas.
Humidity levels are expected to remain noticeable through the evening, slowing the cooling process even after sunset.
Air Quality And Wind Conditions
Air quality levels are currently expected to stay within the satisfactory category across most parts of Hyderabad. Wind speeds are likely to remain moderate at around 10 kmph for most of the day before briefly strengthening if evening storm activity develops.
What Residents Can Expect
Hyderabad's weather on May 10 is expected to follow a familiar late-summer pattern; intense daytime heat followed by slightly unstable evening conditions. While widespread rainfall is not currently expected, changing skies and isolated thunderstorms may briefly affect some areas later in the day.
Residents heading outdoors during afternoon hours are likely to face the harshest conditions, while evening commuters may need to stay alert for sudden gusty winds or brief showers in isolated parts of the city.

articles.title: Telangana rolls out heatwave action plan; schools to implement water bells, cooling centres set up - Dailyhunt
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad: As Telangana prepare for heatwave, all schools have been instructed to implement 'water bells' and malls, religious places and public buildings have been designated as 'cooling centres' for the general public seeking reprieve from the hot sun.
It is now mandatory for schools to provide 'water bells', with breaks to ensure the students are well hydrated. Schools have also been asked not to hold open-air classes, which will ensure children do not come in direct contact with sun. The Aanganwadi centers across Hyderabad and State will now operate only until 11 am
Malls and shops have been instructed by the State government to keep cold water at their premises, while religious places, government buildings, and panchayat offices have been designated as cooling centres, where the public can go and rest during the heat wave. It has also been decided to install green shades at traffic junctions and public gatherings.
Health Minister, Damodar Raja Narasimha issued a comprehensive set of instructions to District Collectors to mitigate the risks of heatwave. Beyond students and the general public, special focus is on 'gig economy workers, with specific outreach planned for auto-drivers, cab drivers, and delivery workers who are at high risk during peak afternoon hours.
All the government hospitals should have a dedicated heatstroke team in the emergency area to begin treatment immediately upon a patient’s arrival. The Minister has also directed the electricity department to frame a summer-specific power load plan to ensure uninterrupted electricity to hospitals and water supply units, while the Fire Department conducts audits of chemical godowns to prevent heat-induced fires.
The Telangana State Development Planning Society (TGDPS) will now provide Mandal-level, two-day forecasts focusing on the Heat Index, which is a combination of temperature and humidity, and to be shared directly to local nodal officers.
Authorities have warned of an 'Orange Alert' condition already prevailing in several districts, with May and early June expected to see sustained maximum temperatures between 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius.
Residents are urged to avoid outdoor activities between 12 noon and 3 pm and utilize the newly established 'Chalivendrams' (drinking water kiosks) being set up across urban and rural congregation points, authorities urged.
- Malls, public buildings and religious places to serve water and cooling centers
- Mandatory water bells in schools and avoid open-air classes
- Aanganwadi centres to be shut down by 11 am
- Shift schedule of outdoor workers from 12 noon to 3 pm during heart wave alert
- Fire department to conduct security check at commercial complexes and hospitals
- Shaded areas and cool water tubs for animals in urban and rural areas
- Veterinary services to treat heat stress animals
